questions.q: In which language did the filmmaker Ritwik Ghatak primarily make his films?
questions.explanation: Ritwik Ghatak was a Bengali filmmaker who made most of his films in the Bengali language.

questions.q: The recurring theme running through many of Ritwik Ghatak's films is the trauma of which historical event?
questions.explanation: Ghatak's films were deeply marked by the trauma of the 1947 Partition that divided Bengal.

questions.q: In which year was the celebrated Ritwik Ghatak film Meghe Dhaka Tara released?
questions.explanation: Ghatak's acclaimed film Meghe Dhaka Tara was released in 1960.

questions.q: What does the title of Ritwik Ghatak's 1960 film Meghe Dhaka Tara translate to in English?
questions.explanation: Meghe Dhaka Tara translates to 'The Cloud-Capped Star' in English.

questions.q: In which year was the filmmaker Ritwik Ghatak born?
questions.explanation: Ritwik Ghatak was born in 1925.

questions.q: In which year did the filmmaker Ritwik Ghatak die?
questions.explanation: Ritwik Ghatak died in 1976.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak is widely regarded as a contemporary and counterpart of which other towering Bengali filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Ghatak is often discussed alongside Satyajit Ray as one of the great masters of Bengali cinema.

questions.q: In which year was Ritwik Ghatak's film Subarnarekha released?
questions.explanation: Subarnarekha was completed in 1962 but released only in 1965.

questions.q: Critical reappraisal of Ritwik Ghatak's work and his major recognition came largely at what point relative to his life?
options: Early in his career, in the 1950s
questions.explanation: Largely under-recognised in his lifetime, Ghatak received major critical reappraisal after his death in 1976.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak's film Subarnarekha takes its title from what kind of geographical feature?
questions.explanation: Subarnarekha is named after the Subarnarekha river, a recurring symbolic presence in the film.

questions.q: Which three films are commonly grouped together as Ritwik Ghatak's 'Partition trilogy'?
options: Nagarik, Bari Theke Paliye and Ajantrik
Titash Ekti Nadir Naam, Jukti Takko Aar Gappo and Ajantrik
Pather Panchali, Aparajito and Apur Sansar
Meghe Dhaka Tara, Komal Gandhar and Subarnarekha
questions.explanation: Meghe Dhaka Tara (1960), Komal Gandhar (1961) and Subarnarekha (1965) form Ghatak's Partition trilogy.

questions.q: What was the title of Ritwik Ghatak's first completed feature film, made around 1952 but released only decades later?
questions.explanation: Nagarik (The Citizen), made around 1952, was Ghatak's first completed feature but was released only after his death.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak's 1958 film Ajantrik is notable for centring on a man's relationship with what?
questions.explanation: Ajantrik depicts a driver's deep, almost human bond with his dilapidated old motor car, which he calls Jagaddal.

questions.q: Which actress played the self-sacrificing heroine Nita in Ritwik Ghatak's Meghe Dhaka Tara (1960)?
questions.explanation: Supriya Choudhury (Supriya Devi) played the long-suffering heroine Nita in Meghe Dhaka Tara.

questions.q: In which present-day country was Ritwik Ghatak born, in the city of Dhaka?
questions.explanation: Ghatak was born in Dhaka, in present-day Bangladesh, then part of undivided Bengal in British India.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak's only feature film shot in present-day Bangladesh, Titash Ekti Nadir Naam, was released in which year?
questions.explanation: Titash Ekti Nadir Naam (A River Called Titash) was released in 1973.

questions.q: Before turning fully to cinema, Ritwik Ghatak was an active member of which leftist cultural movement?
questions.explanation: Ghatak was an active member of the Indian People's Theatre Association (IPTA) as a playwright and actor.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak co-wrote the screenplay for which acclaimed 1958 Bimal Roy Hindi film, a reincarnation drama?
questions.explanation: Ghatak co-wrote the screenplay of Bimal Roy's hit reincarnation drama Madhumati (1958).

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak served as a teacher and later vice-principal at which premier Indian film school?
options: Satyajit Ray Film and Television Institute, Kolkata
Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), Pune
questions.explanation: Ghatak taught at the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune and served as its vice-principal.

questions.q: What is the English title of Ritwik Ghatak's 1961 film Komal Gandhar?
questions.explanation: Komal Gandhar is known in English as 'E-Flat', referencing a musical note.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak frequently drew on which body of imagery, including the Great Mother goddess figure, to give his films a mythic dimension?
questions.explanation: Ghatak infused his films with Hindu mythology and Jungian archetypes, notably the Great Mother goddess figure.

questions.q: What was the title of Ritwik Ghatak's last completed feature film, made in 1974?
questions.explanation: Jukti Takko Aar Gappo (Reason, Debate and a Story), made in 1974, was Ghatak's last completed feature.

questions.q: In Ritwik Ghatak's final film Jukti Takko Aar Gappo (1974), who played the central character, the dishevelled intellectual Nilkantha?
questions.explanation: Ghatak himself played the autobiographical, self-destructive intellectual Nilkantha in Jukti Takko Aar Gappo.

questions.q: Which novel by Advaita Mallabarman did Ritwik Ghatak adapt for his 1973 film Titash Ekti Nadir Naam?
questions.explanation: The film was based on Advaita Mallabarman's novel of the same name, Titash Ekti Nadir Naam.

questions.q: The acclaimed line 'Dada, ami banchte chai' ('Brother, I want to live') is a famous cry from the heroine in which Ritwik Ghatak film?
questions.explanation: The anguished cry 'Dada, ami banchte chai' is uttered by Nita in the climax of Meghe Dhaka Tara.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak wrote the 1957 Hindi film Musafir, which marked the directorial debut of which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Ghatak co-wrote Musafir (1957), which was Hrishikesh Mukherjee's debut as a director; Ghatak wrote it but did not direct it.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak's 1958 children's film Bari Theke Paliye centres on what kind of protagonist?
options: A runaway boy exploring the city of Calcutta
questions.explanation: Bari Theke Paliye (The Runaway) follows a young boy who flees home to explore the wider world of Calcutta.

questions.q: Who is credited as the music composer of Ritwik Ghatak's 1960 film Meghe Dhaka Tara?
questions.explanation: Jyotirindra Moitra is credited as the music director and composer of Meghe Dhaka Tara (1960).

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak was honoured by the Government of India with which civilian award in 1970, during his lifetime?
questions.explanation: Ghatak was awarded the Padma Shri in 1970 for his contribution to cinema.

questions.q: Ritwik Ghatak's only feature shot in then East Pakistan-turned-Bangladesh, Titash Ekti Nadir Naam, portrays the dying way of life of which community?
options: A fishing community along a river
questions.explanation: Titash Ekti Nadir Naam depicts a Malo fishing community whose life withers as the river Titash dries up.
